title: Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors
subtitle: Net [official development assistance](#dod:oda) to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors

Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.

The choropleth map displays net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors for the year 2023, with values expressed in constant 2022 US dollars. The color scale ranges from pale yellow for $0 to dark blue for amounts exceeding $300 million. North America, Australia, and most of Europe are hatched, indicating no data. Across South America, countries such as Brazil and Colombia sit in the $10 million to $30 million band, while Peru and Bolivia appear in the $0 to $10 million range. In Africa, a cluster of nations including Nigeria, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo fall within the $30 million to $100 million band. Several countries in Southern and East Africa, such as Mozambique and Tanzania, are shaded in the darker blue tones, placing them in the $100 million to $300 million or above $300 million categories. In Asia, India is colored in the darkest blue, indicating assistance above $300 million, while Pakistan and Bangladesh are in the $100 million to $300 million band. China and Russia are shown in the lightest yellow band, corresponding to $0. Southeast Asian nations like Indonesia and the Philippines are distributed between the $10 million and $100 million bands. The Middle East shows variation, with countries like Yemen in the $30 million to $100 million range and others in lighter shades.
